title: Wholesome Jammy Irish Buckwheat Casserole
description: A sweet and crisp Irish casserole with buckwheat flour, applesauce, and anti-inflammatory spices, baked in a tandoor clay pot for a kid-friendly bite.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F and lightly oil a tandoor clay pot with olive oil.
In a bowl, whisk together buckwheat flour, rolled oats, ground cinnamon, ground ginger, and salt until evenly distributed.
Add the applesauce, honey, and vanilla extract to the dry mixture and stir until a cohesive batter forms.
Pour the batter into the prepared clay pot, smoothing the surface with a spatula.
Cover the pot tightly with foil and bake for 35 minutes until the top is set and golden brown.
Remove the foil, sprinkle chopped walnuts over the surface, and bake uncovered for an additional 10 minutes to develop a crisp topping.
Allow the casserole to cool in the pot for five minutes before transferring it to a cutting board.
Cut the cooled casserole into squares and serve warm or at room temperature.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.
